notes: Use for shop identity, primary domain, and high-level storefront metadata.
Prefer sortKey and cursors for repeatable pagination through large order histories.
Use the query argument for bounded searches instead of pulling the whole order set.
defaultSelection: edges { cursor node { id name createdAt updatedAt } }

notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Order/<id> when reading one order.
defaultSelection: lineItems(first: 25) { edges { cursor node { id name quantity sku } } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } }

notes: Prefer query and cursors for bounded product scans.
Add inventory or publication fields explicitly when those details matter.
defaultSelection: edges { cursor node { id title handle updatedAt } }

notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Product/<id> when reading one product.
defaultSelection: variants(first: 25) { edges { node { id title sku } } }

notes: Customer queries can be large; prefer first plus cursors and narrow searches.
defaultSelection: edges { cursor node { id displayName email updatedAt } }

notes: Use the gid form gid://shopify/Customer/<id> when reading one customer.
This is the current cleanup-safe proof mutation for validating generic mutation execution.
This is the pinned first-wave root contract for Shopify GraphQL materialization.
It is intentionally smaller than full introspection and should broaden as the package expands.
